signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Bias
signals.text: NZD/CAD at 0.4390 (implied) — NZD dominates as the G8 leader while CAD lags, creating a 1.464% spread. The divergence is driven by RBNZ hike bias vs BoC hold stance, alongside optimism for an Iran peace deal which supports risk-sensitive NZD over oil-linked CAD. NZD COT LF net short -16,833 (23% long) vs RBNZ hike bias indicates significant short-squeeze risk if the May 27 meeting confirms hawkishness.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence + Geopolitical
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7262 — COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) vs retail 90% short creates a massive institutional vs retail divergence. Retail is aggressively fading the risk-on rotation triggered by Iran peace deal hopes and the 4.35% RBA carry advantage (-60bp vs Fed). Extreme retail short positioning suggests a capitulation squeeze toward multi-year tops if peace talks progress.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Retail Extreme + Carry Divergence
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7773 — Retail 85% long (extreme) vs COT LF net short -6,117 (33.6% long). While the 375bp Fed-SNB carry premium structurally favors USD, the retail crowd is caught long during a 2.99% monthly decline. Peace deal optimism reduces safe-haven CHF demand, but retail over-concentration long suggests further downside before a sustainable carry-driven reversal.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + ECB Stance
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1776 — Correlation with EuroStoxx at +0.729 vs norm -0.130 (z=+1.84σ) indicates EUR is trading as a pure risk-on proxy rather than a funding currency. ECB’s Villeroy emphasizes data-dependence, keeping the vs USD relevant. Watch for a break in equity momentum to test if the EUR/USD 1W +0.40% gain can sustain without risk-on tailwinds.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry vs Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.36 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid, but JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) remains crowded. 160.00 remains the key level for BoJ verbal warnings. Peace deal headlines and falling WTI oil ($92.4) provide a breather for JPY, but the carry advantage dominates until VIX exceeds 25. Watch the next BoJ meeting (mkt: 40% hike priced) for a shift in the 0.75% policy rate.
